metadata.notes: Trivia and quotes researched via web. Quotes are paraphrased/translated where indicated; only verifiable statements included.

vips.name: M. K. Stalin
vips.result_status: Trailing (margin 7,544 vs V.S. Babu, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Eighth and incumbent Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu since 2021; third son of DMK patriarch M. Karunanidhi and the architect of the DMK's 'Dravidian Model' governance pitch.
famous_quotes: Whenever our mother tongue, Tamil, was under threat, we fought relentlessly to protect it. Even today, under the guise of the three-language policy, efforts are on to impose Hindi. (2025)
Do not confuse NEET with educational ability — they are different. NEET benefits students of a particular stream.
History tells us fascists, who construed the silence of people as fear, ultimately fell.
trivia: Named after Soviet leader Joseph Stalin by Karunanidhi, who was attending a condolence meeting for the dictator just four days before his son was born on 1 March 1953.
Jailed under MISA in Madras Central Prison in 1976 during the Emergency at age 23; says fellow detainee C. Chittibabu died of torture in custody.
Spent over five decades climbing the DMK ladder — youth wing chief, Chennai's first directly elected Mayor (1996), Deputy CM (2009-11) — before becoming CM at age 68.
Briefly acted in films and a tele-serial ('Ore Ratham', 1980s) before fully committing to politics; nicknamed 'Thalapathy' by party cadres.
Has won the Kolathur seat since its creation in 2008, three times in a row before 2026.
recent_news_2025_2026: Faced an unprecedented Kolathur challenge from TVK candidate V.S. Babu in 2026.
Welcomed long-time rival O. Panneerselvam into DMK in February 2026, calling him 'a dear brother'.
Released autobiography 'Ungalil Oruvan' (One Among You); pushed back hard against the three-language policy and delimitation through 2025.
Elevated son Udhayanidhi to Deputy CM in September 2024, drawing 'son rise / dynasty' criticism.

vips.name: Edappadi K. Palaniswami (EPS)
vips.party: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (AIADMK)
vips.result_status: Leading by 78,058 votes (vs Premkumar K, Independent)
vips.famous_for: Former Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu (2017-2021), AIADMK General Secretary, and Leader of Opposition; widely seen as the inheritor of Jayalalithaa's organisational machine.
famous_quotes: Vijay may be a great actor, but we are the best politicians. (2025-26)
If there is an alternative to the DMK, it is only the AIADMK. (2026)
Vijay went without planning; that is why 41 lives were lost. He did not visit the victims or console the families. We did. How can he be a leader? (on the Karur stampede, 2025)
trivia: From a Kongu Vellalar Gounder farming family in Salem; was a jaggery trader before politics, earning him the nickname 'Vellam (jaggery) Palaniswami'.
Also ran a stone-crushing unit and a small bus service in Salem before fully turning to politics in 1974.
Was V.K. Sasikala's hand-picked choice to replace her as Legislature Party leader in February 2017, but later led the council that expelled her and TTV Dhinakaran.
Has won Edappadi five times in a row; in 2021 he won by 93,802 votes.
Took control of AIADMK after a bitter dual-leadership tussle with O. Panneerselvam, expelling OPS in July 2022.
recent_news_2025_2026: Re-stitched a BJP-AIADMK alliance for 2026 after a four-year estrangement; insisted on contesting as the alliance's CM face.
Repeatedly rejected calls to readmit OPS or Sasikala into AIADMK.
Sparred publicly with TVK chief Vijay through 2025, attacking him over the September 2025 Karur stampede that killed 41.
Faced a major upset narrative in 2026 as AIADMK trailed TVK in many segments, even as he himself led comfortably in Edappadi.

vips.name: O. Panneerselvam (OPS)
vips.party: Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) — joined Feb 2026 from AIADMK (expelled 2022)
vips.result_status: Leading by 2,590 votes (vs Prakash S, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Three-time Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu and Jayalalithaa's most visible loyalist — known for taking the CM chair as a 'caretaker' whenever Amma was indisposed or jailed, and for his trademark public prostrations before her.
famous_quotes: I thank CM and DMK President Stalin for accepting me to join DMK. I have joined DMK with full joy. EPS is behaving as a dictator. (Feb 2026)
Ready to quit politics if allegations are proven. (2026)
Dharmam thalai kaakkum (Righteousness will protect us) — his oft-repeated catchphrase from his 2017 'Dharma Yutham' revolt against Sasikala.
trivia: Ran a tea stall in Periyakulam, Theni district, before politics — earning him the lasting 'tea-vendor turned CM' tag.
Sat in dharna at Jayalalithaa's MGR Memorial in February 2017, refusing to back Sasikala — a televised one-man rebellion that split AIADMK overnight.
Was Jayalalithaa's 'stand-in CM' in 2001, 2014 and 2016 — three short tenures totaling under two years.
From the powerful Mukkulathor (Thevar) community of southern Tamil Nadu.
Expelled by AIADMK general council on 11 July 2022; spent four years in political wilderness before joining DMK.
recent_news_2025_2026: Joined DMK in February 2026 in the presence of CM Stalin and Deputy CM Udhayanidhi at Anna Arivalayam — a stunning crossover.
Allotted Bodinayakanur (which he won in 2021 as an independent backed by TVK-aligned forces) on the DMK 'Rising Sun' symbol for 2026.
His sons and supporters were inducted into DMK alongside him.
Faced fresh corruption allegations in 2025-26.

vips.name: Udhayanidhi Stalin
vips.result_status: Leading by 4,319 votes (vs Selvam D, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Tamil Nadu's third Deputy Chief Minister (since September 2024), DMK youth-wing secretary, and a film actor-producer; son of CM M. K. Stalin and great-grandson-equivalent inheritor of the Karunanidhi political family.
famous_quotes: Sanatana Dharma should be eradicated, similar to the way malaria and dengue need to be eradicated. (September 2023, at the Eradication of Sanatana conference)
Sanatana cannot be simply opposed, but had to be eradicated… it is against social justice and equality.
trivia: Founded Red Giant Movies in 2008; the company's first release was Vijay's 'Kuruvi' (2008) — striking irony given Vijay is now his political rival.
Made his acting debut in 'Oru Kal Oru Kannadi' (2012) and won the Filmfare Award for Best Male Debut – South.
Belongs to the fourth generation of the Karunanidhi political family; his last film as actor was 'Maamannan' (2023).
His 2023 Sanatana Dharma remarks triggered nationwide FIRs and a Supreme Court rebuke.
Affidavit filings show his personal income dropped sharply after he gave up films, while his wife Kiruthiga's (a director) declared assets rose significantly.
recent_news_2025_2026: Sworn in as Deputy CM on 28 September 2024 — BJP and AIADMK called it a 'Son Rise' / dynastic move.
Led DMK's 2026 youth-vote campaign and digital outreach, often the public face countering Vijay's TVK pitch.
Faced repeated AIADMK and BJP attacks on his Sanatana remarks throughout 2025.
Filed nomination from Chepauk-Thiruvallikeni in April 2026 — the seat once held by his grandfather Karunanidhi.

vips.name: C. Joseph Vijay (Thalapathy Vijay)
vips.result_status: Leading by 25,723 votes in Perambur (vs R.D. Shekar, DMK); leading by 13,879 in Tiruchirappalli East
vips.famous_for: Tamil cinema's reigning superstar 'Thalapathy', founder-president of TVK (launched February 2024); his 2026 debut shattered the DMK-AIADMK Dravidian duopoly with TVK leading 100+ seats in early counting.
famous_quotes: Under the guise of the Dravidian Model, they (DMK) are misleading people as an anti-people government. (27 October 2024, first state conference at Vikravandi)
I would take everything from Periyar except his anti-God stance. (2024)
I, who have come to ask justice for you, also want justice. (2025, on the Karur stampede investigation)
Politics is a battlefield.
trivia: Born Joseph Vijay Chandrasekhar on 22 June 1974; father S.A. Chandrasekhar is a film director and mother Shoba is a playback singer.
Made his on-screen debut as a child actor in his father's film 'Vetri' (1984); broke through as a hero with 'Thirumalai' (2003).
Declared BJP-RSS as TVK's 'ideological enemy' and DMK as the 'electoral enemy' — a deliberately two-front positioning.
His 27 September 2025 TVK rally in Karur ended in a crowd crush that killed 41 and injured around 100; CBI grilled him for 13 hours.
Despite being one of India's highest-paid actors with 69 lead films, he stopped doing films after 'GOAT' (2024) to focus on politics.
Contested from two seats simultaneously in 2026 — Perambur (Chennai) and Tiruchirappalli East.
recent_news_2025_2026: Karur stampede on 27 September 2025 became the defining controversy of his political launch.
Questioned twice by CBI in Delhi (January 2026) and once in Chennai over crowd-management failures.
Shifted from mega rallies to closed-door meetings post-Karur, before returning with a big Salem event.
TVK contested all 234 seats alone and emerged as the single largest party in early 2026 trends.

vips.name: Dr. L. Murugan
vips.result_status: Trailing by 7,484 votes (vs Kamali S, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Union Minister of State for Information & Broadcasting and Parliamentary Affairs; former TN BJP president (2020-2023) and the first person from the Arunthathiyar (Dalit) community to become a Union Minister since Independence.
famous_quotes: DMK always wants to divide people. Their politics is one of division and not of unity and integrity. Sanatana is one of the bedrocks of Indian nationhood. (April 2024)
DMK is trying to show the BJP is against Tamils but it'll never happen; the BJP has grown well in Tamil Nadu. (October 2024)
He (A. Raja) has never been for the Hindu. He has always abused us. He has always abused our God. (2024 Nilgiris campaign)
trivia: Born 29 May 1977 in Konur, Namakkal district; PhD from University of Madras and law degree from Tamil Nadu Dr. Ambedkar Law College.
Joined ABVP in 1997, then RSS, where he rose to become national general secretary of the SC division before BJP.
Served as Vice-Chairman of the National Commission for Scheduled Castes (2017-2020) before becoming TN BJP chief in March 2020.
Has had a thin electoral record — got just 1,800 votes in Rasipuram (2011), lost Dharapuram by 1,393 votes in 2021; entered Parliament via MP Rajya Sabha in 2022.
Lost the 2024 Nilgiris Lok Sabha contest to DMK heavyweight A. Raja by a large margin.
recent_news_2025_2026: Fielded from Avanashi (SC) in April 2026 as one of 27 BJP candidates within the AIADMK-BJP alliance.
Continued as Union MoS through 2025-26; the BJP's lead Tamil-language attack voice on the Sanatana Dharma row.
Trailed TVK's Kamali S in early Avanashi counting trends, mirroring BJP's broader poor showing.
Central to BJP's 'Dharma vs Adharma' framing in TN, including the 'War of Gods' narrative around Murugan-the-deity in 2024.

vips.name: Premalatha Vijayakanth
vips.result_status: Leading by 3,301 votes (vs Vijay S, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Widow of 'Captain' Vijayakanth and General Secretary of DMDK since December 2023; the woman holding together DMDK after the founder's death, often called 'Anni' (sister-in-law) by cadres.
famous_quotes: Brother Stalin will be CM again. (April 2026)
Those who have the patience to work must also have the patience to wait. There is no need for excessive expectations based on post-poll surveys. (May 2026)
EPS stabbed us in the back over the alliance deal. (September 2025)
trivia: Born 18 March 1969; was a national-level shot put and discus throw athlete before marriage.
Married actor-politician Vijayakanth on 31 January 1990; holds a B.A. in English Literature.
Became DMDK treasurer in October 2018, then General Secretary in 2023 — formally taking over the party after Captain's death on 28 December 2023.
Sister of LK Sudhish, a film producer; her family's cinema connections sustained DMDK's celebrity-driven fan base.
Won Vriddhachalam in 2021 — the same constituency Vijayakanth first won from in 2011.
Criticised by old DMDK loyalists for centralising party control; her son Vijaya Prabhakaran was also fielded in Virudhunagar in 2026.
recent_news_2025_2026: DMDK initially aligned with AIADMK-BJP for 2026, but the alliance collapsed after Premalatha publicly accused EPS of betrayal in September 2025.
DMDK eventually joined the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance and was given 10 seats.
Led 'legacy revival' campaigns through 2025 invoking Vijayakanth's image, songs and 'Naalai Namadhe' slogan.
Led by 2,400 votes in early Vriddhachalam counting on 4 May 2026.

vips.name: Sowmiya Anbumani
vips.result_status: Leading by 15,176 votes (vs Sivan M, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Daughter-in-law of PMK founder S. Ramadoss and wife of former Union Health Minister Anbumani Ramadoss; environmentalist-turned-politician making her assembly debut in PMK's Vanniyar heartland.
famous_quotes: Instead of getting trapped by 'bad guys' who try to misuse your pictures, inform your parents. You might get scolded, a few beatings, and stay at home for 10 days. (To young women, 2024 Lok Sabha campaign in Dharmapuri)
trivia: Holds a doctorate in Sociology and Environmental Sciences from Madurai Kamaraj University (2019); UG at Stella Maris College, Chennai.
Daughter of M. Krishnasamy, a lawyer and former TN Congress president and ex-MP — politics runs on both sides.
Long associated with Pasumai Thaayagam, the PMK-linked environmental NGO; attended the 2002 Johannesburg Earth Summit and 2009 Copenhagen Climate Summit.
Father-in-law S. Ramadoss has publicly blamed her as 'one of the reasons for the split' in PMK in 2025.
Lost her 2024 Lok Sabha bid from Dharmapuri (NDA-supported) by over 1 lakh votes.
recent_news_2025_2026: Caught in the middle of the bitter Ramadoss vs Anbumani PMK split through 2025; her father-in-law named her publicly as a cause.
Anbumani floated a parallel PMK faction; she campaigned with him while the senior Ramadoss faction (Ayya Pattali Makkal Katchi) opposed her candidature.
Fielded as PMK's Dharmapuri candidate in March 2026; leading on 4 May 2026.

vips.name: K.A. Sengottaiyan
vips.party: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) — expelled from AIADMK Oct 2025
vips.result_status: Leading by 15,852 votes (vs Nallasivam N, DMK)
vips.famous_for: Nine-time MLA from Gobichettipalayam and one of AIADMK's tallest Kongu-belt Gounder leaders; former Tamil Nadu School Education Minister, expelled by EPS in October 2025 and now contesting on Vijay's TVK ticket.
famous_quotes: The violence (in Vachathi) was fabricated and the raid was justified, because the villagers were criminal sandalwood smugglers. (As Forest Minister in TN Assembly, 1992, on the Vachathi atrocities case)
There is no question of going back. I am with Vijay; TVK will form the next government. (27 November 2025, after joining TVK)
trivia: First elected MLA in 1977 from Sathyamangalam at age 29; has won Gobichettipalayam in 1980, 1984, 1989, 1991, 2006, 2011, 2016 and 2021.
Held the School Education and Youth Welfare & Sports portfolio under EPS (2017-2021); his tenure pushed controversial state-board curriculum changes for NEET.
Removed from his ministerial post in 2012 by Jayalalithaa over allegations of an extramarital affair — one of the few AIADMK ministers ever sacked on personal-conduct grounds.
His name is permanently linked to the 1992 Vachathi case where 18 tribal women were raped by forest/police personnel.
From the dominant Kongu Vellalar Gounder community; expelled after meeting Amit Shah in September 2025 and demanding AIADMK reunification.
recent_news_2025_2026: Stripped of all party posts by EPS in late September 2025 and formally expelled from AIADMK on 31 October 2025.
Resigned as Gobichettipalayam MLA and joined TVK at Vijay's office on 27 November 2025 at age 77 — TVK's biggest catch from AIADMK.
EPS held a counter-rally in Gobichettipalayam in early 2026 calling him a 'betrayer'.

vips.name: S.P. Velumani
vips.party: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (AIADMK)
vips.result_status: Leading by 9,220 votes (vs N.R. Karthikeyan, DMK)
vips.famous_for: Four-time Thondamuthur MLA, former Minister for Municipal Administration, Rural Development and Law, and EPS's most powerful Kongu-belt lieutenant; facing some of the largest disproportionate-assets cases in Tamil Nadu.
famous_quotes: A leader who cannot protect his own party workers cannot be expected to protect the people. (Coimbatore, March 2026, attacking Vijay over Karur stampede)
It was Annamalai who caused my party to sever ties with the BJP. (To The Federal, 2024)
trivia: Full name Sugunapuram Palanisamy Velumani; born 10 May 1969, a Kongu Vellalar Gounder and a key EPS confidant.
DVAC searches in March 2022 across 59 locations linked to him recovered roughly 11 kg of gold and documents tied to several cryptocurrencies.
DVAC's 'final opportunity notice' (April 2023) revised the disproportionate-assets quantum from Rs 58.2 crore to about Rs 730 crore — among the largest such figures alleged against any TN politician.
Booked in a Rs 500 crore LED street-lights tender case (Sept 2022) and a Rs 98.25 crore Coimbatore Corporation contract-fraud case.
Held five portfolios simultaneously under EPS, earning the nickname 'super minister'.
recent_news_2025_2026: Faced fresh DVAC chargesheets in 2025 in the LED and Coimbatore Corporation tender-fraud cases.
One of EPS's principal campaigners against Vijay/TVK and DMK in the Kongu belt.
Vocal critic of K. Annamalai through 2024-25, blaming him for the AIADMK-BJP alliance breakdown.

vips.name: M.R. Vijayabhaskar
vips.party: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (AIADMK)
vips.result_status: Leading by 5,843 votes (vs Aasee M. Thiagarajan, DMK)
vips.famous_for: Former Tamil Nadu Transport Minister (2016-21) under Jayalalithaa and EPS; Karur strongman who introduced the rule mandating drivers carry their licences, arrested in 2024 in a Rs 100-crore land grab case.
famous_quotes: The DMK closed our Amma Mini Clinics and rebranded the same scheme as Mudhalvar Marundhagams to divert attention from governance failures. (February 2025)
trivia: Won Karur as AIADMK candidate in 2011, 2016 and 2021; appointed Transport Minister by Jayalalithaa in May 2016 — his very first cabinet post.
Made it compulsory for drivers across Tamil Nadu to carry their driving licence at all times during his term.
Arrested by CBCID in Kerala on 16 July 2024 in a Rs 100 crore land-grabbing case.
DVAC raided 21 properties linked to him on 22 July 2021 and filed an FIR alleging assets of Rs 27.22 crore beyond known income.
EPS personally instructed him to visit injured at Karur hospitals after the 27 September 2025 TVK rally crowd-crush in his home turf.
recent_news_2025_2026: Re-fielded by AIADMK from Karur for 2026 despite multiple pending cases.
Front-and-centre of AIADMK's response to the Karur stampede.
Continues to face DVAC summons and the Kerala-arrest land-grab probe.

vips.name: C. Vijayabaskar
vips.party: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (AIADMK)
vips.result_status: Leading by 37,214 votes (vs Murugesan P, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Doctor-politician and Tamil Nadu's Health Minister from 2013 to 2021; central accused in the multi-crore Gutka Scam and notorious for his on-camera 'you look beautiful' remark to a woman journalist.
famous_quotes: Your specs are nice... Today, you look beautiful. You look beautiful. (To a woman reporter on camera, 2017; later apologised)
The corruption charges against me are politically motivated. (Interview with The Federal, ahead of 2026)
trivia: Born 8 April 1974 at Iluppur, Pudukkottai; MBBS from Rajah Muthiah Medical College, Chidambaram — one of few qualified doctors to head TN Health Ministry.
First elected MLA from Pudukkottai in 2001 at age 27; has won Viralimalai in 2011, 2016 and 2021.
Income Tax raids on his properties in April 2017 unearthed evidence that led the Election Commission to cancel the high-profile R.K. Nagar bye-poll (Jayalalithaa's seat).
Named in CBI chargesheets in the Gutka Scam — Jayam Industries owner Madhava Rao confessed to paying him Rs 56 lakh in bribes.
DVAC has booked him for amassing disproportionate assets of about Rs 27 crore during his 2016-21 tenure.
recent_news_2025_2026: Re-nominated by AIADMK from Viralimalai for 2026; framed all corruption probes as DMK political vendetta.
Continued court appearances through 2025 in CBI Gutka Scam and DVAC cases.
Active AIADMK campaigner across Pudukkottai-Sivaganga belt during 2026.

vips.name: P. Thangamani
vips.party: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (AIADMK)
vips.result_status: Trailing by 1,927 votes (vs C. Vijayalakshmi, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Former Tamil Nadu Minister for Electricity, Prohibition and Excise (2016-21) and earlier Industries Minister (2011-16); Namakkal-belt strongman and one of EPS's core inner-circle ministers.
famous_quotes: There will be no power cuts in the future also. (As Electricity Minister, 2018, denying Stalin's allegations of unscheduled outages)
No power cuts are being carried out under the cover of maintenance works. (TN Assembly, 2018)
DMK will never make it to the Assembly. (Public meeting, June 2018 — a prediction that aged badly when DMK won 2021)
trivia: Born 25 August 1961 in Govindhampalayam village, Namakkal district; rose from a grassroots cadre to senior cabinet minister.
Held the rare combination of Electricity, Prohibition and Excise — covering both TANGEDCO and the entire TASMAC liquor monopoly.
DVAC raided properties linked to him on 15 December 2021 in a disproportionate-assets case.
Opened the 'Minnagam' 24x7 grievance-redressal centre for electricity complaints — still cited as one of his administrative legacies.
Publicly clashed with successor DMK Electricity Minister V. Senthil Balaji in 2022-24 over EB tariff hikes.
recent_news_2025_2026: AIADMK's lead spokesperson on power-sector issues, attacking DMK over EB tariff hikes through 2024-25.
Re-fielded from Kumarapalayam by EPS for 2026.
DVAC disproportionate-assets case remained active through 2025-26.

vips.name: S. Jayakumar
vips.party: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (AIADMK)
vips.result_status: Leading by 5,236 votes (vs Thoppu N.D. Venkatachalam, DMK)
vips.famous_for: First-time AIADMK MLA from Perundurai (won in 2021), an Erode-district businessman and AIADMK organisational hand. (Note: distinct from D. Jayakumar of Royapuram, the AIADMK spokesperson and former Fisheries Minister.)
trivia: Listed his profession as 'business' in his 2021 election affidavit; was around 41 at first election win.
Won Perundurai in 2021 with 85,125 votes (44.84% vote share), defeating KMDK's K.K.C. Balu by 14,507.
Is NOT the former Fisheries Minister — that role was held by D. Jayakumar of Royapuram, contesting Royapuram in 2026.
Operates within AIADMK's Erode Kongu-belt unit, an area squeezed between TVK (after Sengottaiyan's defection) and DMK.
Positioned as an EPS loyalist through the AIADMK leadership turmoil of 2022-25.
recent_news_2025_2026: Renominated by AIADMK from Perundurai for the 2026 election.
Held the seat in early counting trends on 4 May 2026.
Campaigned heavily defending the AIADMK turf in Erode-Kongu after Sengottaiyan's defection to TVK.

vips.name: T.M. Anbarasan
vips.result_status: Trailing by 17,087 votes (vs M. Harish, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Three-term MLA from Alandur and Tamil Nadu's Minister for MSME, Rural Industries, Cottage and Small Industries; the DMK's Kancheepuram district secretary credited with steering the state's small-industries push.
trivia: Holds MSME, Rural Industries, Cottage and Small Industries portfolios plus the TN Slum Clearance Board and Urban Habitat Development Board since May 2021.
Previously served as Minister for Labour Welfare in the 2006-2011 DMK government under Karunanidhi.
Steered the DPR (May 2025) for India's first dedicated gold-jewellery manufacturing park at SIDCO Industrial Estate, Kurichi, Coimbatore — a Rs 126 crore project.
Launched a Rs 100 crore credit guarantee scheme in April 2025 for small industries.
Long-serving DMK district secretary of Kancheepuram.
recent_news_2025_2026: Announced completion of DPR for the Coimbatore gold-jewellery manufacturing park in May 2025.
Department reported mentoring 66,018 new entrepreneurs and disbursing Rs 5,490 crore in MSME loans between 2021 and mid-2025.
Re-nominated by DMK from Alandur for 2026.

vips.name: Anbil Mahesh Poyyamozhi
vips.result_status: Leading by 2,034 votes (vs Vijayakumar A Navalpattu S. Viji, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Tamil Nadu's School Education Minister and the public face of the state's opposition to the National Education Policy 2020; a third-generation Dravidian leader and close confidant of Udhayanidhi Stalin.
famous_quotes: Compared the National Education Policy 2020 to a 'rogue elephant' (Madhayaanai) — central metaphor of his 2025 Tamil book 'NEP Ennum Madhayaanai', released by CM Stalin in May 2025.
trivia: Third-generation DMK politician: grandson of veteran DMK minister Anbil P. Dharmalingam and son of former MLA Anbil Poyyamozhi.
A medical doctor by training (the 'Dr.' in his social handles), he waited 16 years for a ticket before being fielded in Thiruverumbur in 2016 and 2021.
Serves as DMK district secretary for Tiruchirappalli (South).
Authored 'NEP 2020 Ennum Madhayaanai' (2025), a book-length critique of the Centre's education policy.
Long-time personal friend of DMK scion and Deputy CM Udhayanidhi Stalin.
recent_news_2025_2026: Released his anti-NEP book through CM Stalin in May 2025, framing the DMK's education narrative for 2026.
Heavily defended the state's PM-POSHAN-replacement breakfast scheme through 2025.
Nominated by DMK for a third consecutive term from Thiruverumbur.

vips.name: Anitha R. Radhakrishnan
vips.result_status: Leading by 5,302 votes (vs J. Murugan, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Veteran Thoothukudi-district politician and Tamil Nadu's Fisheries, Fishermen Welfare and Animal Husbandry Minister; the public face of the state's response to Sri Lankan Navy attacks on Tamil fishermen.
famous_quotes: CM M.K. Stalin is of the view that talks between India and Sri Lanka are necessary to have a permanent solution to the attacks on Tamil fishermen by the Sri Lankan Navy. (2022, re-iterated through 2024-25)
trivia: Has won Tiruchendur six times — first as AIADMK candidate (2001, 2006), then as DMK candidate (2009 by-poll, 2011, 2016, 2021) after being expelled from AIADMK in July 2009.
Was Minister for Animal Husbandry and later Housing & Urban Development in Jayalalithaa's 2001 AIADMK government before defecting to DMK.
Drew nationwide attention when video showed fishermen carrying him into the sea on their shoulders so his shoes wouldn't get wet.
Faces a long-running disproportionate-assets case originally registered by DVAC in 2019; ED has attached assets worth roughly Rs 6.5 crore under PMLA.
Madras High Court granted an interim stay in April 2026 on trial proceedings before the Thoothukudi court.
recent_news_2025_2026: ED attached fresh properties worth Rs 1.26 crore in January 2025 in the ongoing money-laundering probe.
Handled coastal-fishermen agitations at Thangachimadam (Rameswaram) in March 2025 over Sri Lankan Navy arrests.
Madras HC stayed his DA-case trial in April 2026 just before the polls.
Re-nominated by DMK from Tiruchendur.

vips.name: R. Sakkarapani
vips.result_status: Leading by 28,835 votes (vs Vidiyal S. Sekar, BJP)
vips.famous_for: Six-term DMK MLA from Oddanchatram (1996, 2001, 2006, 2011, 2016, 2021) and Tamil Nadu's Minister for Food, Civil Supplies, Consumer Protection and Price Control — the man in charge of the state's PDS and ration network.
trivia: Has won Oddanchatram in Dindigul district six times in a row — an unbroken three-decade record.
Served as Chief Government Whip of the TN Assembly in 2006-2011 and as DMK Whip across 2011-2016 and 2016-2021.
His ministry oversees Tamil Nadu's universal PDS — among the largest in India — including paddy procurement and the free-rice scheme.
Has repeatedly clashed publicly with the Centre over wheat allocation to TN ration shops.
Faced AIADMK and BJP attacks through 2024-25 over alleged ration-card printing delays and an alleged Rs 100 crore pulse-procurement scam ahead of Deepavali 2024.
recent_news_2025_2026: Tamil Nadu PDS ran short of wheat in November 2025; he assured supplies by mid-November and said the state had been 'continuously requesting' the Centre.
Defended his department in 2025 against allegations that ration-card printing had been halted since May.
Re-nominated by DMK from Oddanchatram.

vips.name: S. Regupathy
vips.result_status: Leading by 1,357 votes (vs Vairamuthu PK, AIADMK)
vips.famous_for: Veteran Pudukkottai-district lawyer-politician and Tamil Nadu's former Minister for Law, Courts, Prisons and Prevention of Corruption (2021-2025); subsequently held the Mines and Minerals portfolio.
trivia: Born 30 July 1950 in Pudukkottai; an advocate (B.L. from College of Law, Chennai, 1973).
Represents Thirumayam in Pudukkottai district and is one of the senior-most legal voices in the DMK cabinet.
Held the Law, Courts, Prisons and Prevention of Corruption portfolio from May 2021; later moved to Minerals and Mines.
As Law Minister, was the government's chief defender on legal-confrontation matters with the Governor and the Centre.
Family is associated with the Karpaga Vinayaka educational trust in the Pudukkottai region.
recent_news_2025_2026: Featured prominently in 2025 as Law Minister defending DMK on Governor R.N. Ravi's holding of bills.
Reshuffled out of Law in 2025 and given Minerals and Mines.
Re-nominated by DMK from Thirumayam.

vips.name: K. Selvaperunthagai
vips.result_status: Trailing by 23,957 votes (vs K. Thennarasu, TVK)
vips.famous_for: President of the Tamil Nadu Congress Committee (TNCC) and sitting Sriperumbudur MLA — the first Dalit to lead the TNCC in over four decades and Congress's chief negotiator with DMK for 2026.
famous_quotes: The alliance between the DMK and the Congress is natural, very intact… We are only talking and negotiating with the DMK. (February 2026)
People of Tamil Nadu are far away from the ideology of the BJP and RSS… Even if Modi comes to Tamil Nadu 100 times, he won't take anything from Tamil Nadu. (February 2026)
Alleged 'Hindi-speaking Income Tax officials' had 'unlawfully confined' him during Rahul Gandhi's TN visit. (April 2026)
trivia: Born 14 April 1964 at Manimangalam village in Sriperumbudur; B.L. from Sri Venkateswara University, Tirupati and M.A. from Madras University.
Took charge as TNCC President on 21 February 2024 — first Dalit chief in over 40 years.
Currently elected leader of the Congress Legislature Party in the TN Assembly, having won Sriperumbudur in 2021.
Earlier headed the TNCC's SC Department as State Chairman.
Was at the centre of an alleged 'discrimination' incident at the Vallakottai temple consecration in 2024.
recent_news_2025_2026: Led Congress's seat-sharing talks with DMK for 2026, eventually settling in the high-20s of seats.
April 2026: alleged that IT officials raided his Sriperumbudur residence and 'confined' him during Rahul Gandhi's TN campaign visit; CBDT denied any search.
Released CCTV footage on 24 April 2026 to back his claim.
Reports in May 2026 suggested he had told colleagues he would step down as TNCC chief after the result.

vips.name: Ezhilan Naganathan
vips.result_status: Leading by 7,536 votes (vs Prabhakar J.C.D, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Doctor-turned-DMK-MLA from Thousand Lights (Chennai) and the party's most aggressive parliamentary voice on anti-NEET, anti-Hindi-imposition, state autonomy and the demand to put medical education back on the State List.
famous_quotes: Budget allocation has been an issue and we have our own language policy, which is constitutionally mandated… without understanding the Constitution, the union minister is making a very sarcastic comment… this is a dictator model. (February 2025, on the Centre's language stance)
People who study Hindi sell panipuri, pakoda. (2025 — drew sharp criticism)
Lotus will never bloom as long as we eat beef here. (2025, attacking BJP)
trivia: Born 27 June 1979; MD in General Medicine from Madras Medical College (2007), gold medallist; winner of B. Braun Medical Award and Edward Norraday Award.
Defeated BJP's Khushboo Sundar in Thousand Lights in 2021 in one of Chennai's most-watched contests.
Long-standing public campaigner against NEET in Tamil Nadu; vocal supporter of the Tamil Eelam cause.
Member of the Tamil Nadu State Planning Commission.
Submitted a 6-point charter (August 2025) recommending medical education be moved back from the Concurrent List to the State List.
recent_news_2025_2026: Issued a 6-point state-autonomy charter in August 2025 demanding repeal of the NMC Act 2019.
Repeatedly attacked the Centre through 2025 over alleged 'subtle Hindi imposition' via Hindi names for centrally sponsored schemes.
Made headlines for an anti-Hindi/anti-BJP speech in 2025 that opponents said insulted Hindi-speakers and Hindus.
Re-nominated by DMK from Thousand Lights.

vips.name: E.V. Velu
vips.result_status: Leading by 291 votes (vs Arul Arumugam, TVK)
vips.famous_for: Three-term DMK MLA from Tiruvannamalai (2011, 2016, 2021) and Tamil Nadu's Minister for Public Works, Highways and Minor Ports; full name Ethirajulu Vajjaravelu, a Telugu-origin leader who has become one of the cabinet's loudest Tamil-pride voices.
famous_quotes: Who the hell even understands that? (4 July 2025, Vellore — mocking Sanskrit mantras while criticising the Centre's Rs 2,500 crore Sanskrit allocation against Rs 167 crore for Tamil)
trivia: Holds the PWD, Highways and Minor Ports portfolios since May 2021 in the Stalin ministry.
Born into a Telugu-speaking family in Tiruvannamalai, which opponents repeatedly cite when he champions Tamil-language causes; full name Ethirajulu Vajjaravelu.
Owns the CBSE-affiliated Jeeva Velu International School in Mathur, Tiruvannamalai — used by critics to allege hypocrisy on the DMK's anti-Hindi/anti-CBSE line.
Note: he is NOT the son of DMK general secretary Duraimurugan — Duraimurugan's son is DMK Vellore MP Kathir Anand.
His son E.V. Kumaran's properties were searched by the Income-Tax Department in November 2023.
recent_news_2025_2026: February 2025: faced fresh ridicule over the 'Telugu-speaking minister claiming Tamil is under threat' line and over Hindi being taught at his CBSE school.
July 2025: nationally-reported speech in Vellore mocking Sanskrit wedding mantras.
TVK executive issued an apology to party president Vijay in 2025 after inviting Velu to a function — used by opponents as a sign of cross-party discomfort with him.
Re-nominated by DMK from Tiruvannamalai.

vips.name: M. Thamimun Ansari
vips.party: Manithaneya Jananayaga Katchi (MJK) — DMK alliance, contesting on Rising Sun symbol
vips.result_status: Leading by 4,179 votes (vs Pandian K.A, AIADMK)
vips.famous_for: Founder-president of the Manithaneya Jananayaga Katchi (MJK), former Nagapattinam MLA and one of Tamil Nadu's senior Muslim political voices; in 2026 contesting Chidambaram on the DMK's Rising Sun symbol.
famous_quotes: (February 2026, on Congress-DMK seat-sharing tensions): Urged Congress 'not to speak on social media' but to take their demands privately to Arivalayam or Sathyamoorthy Bhavan.
(February 2026): Expressed confidence the DMK alliance 'would win over 200 assembly seats' in 2026.
trivia: Founded the Manithaneya Jananayaga Katchi (MJK) in 2015 after splitting from M.H. Jawahirullah's Manithaneya Makkal Katchi (MMK).
Was MLA from Nagapattinam (won in 2016 as MMK candidate before forming MJK).
His shift to the DMK alliance was treated as a setback to AIADMK's minority outreach.
MJK was allotted one seat — Chidambaram — in the 2026 DMK alliance arrangement.
Frequently positions MJK as a 'secular Muslim' alternative to outfits like SDPI and AIMIM in TN.
recent_news_2025_2026: Through 2025-26 publicly mediated Congress-DMK alliance frictions.
Announced as MJK's sole DMK-alliance candidate from Chidambaram for 2026, contesting on the Rising Sun symbol.
